Lord Richard Welert I

memoryArtifacts.description:
Richard's mother was Terry Lynn Wells, b. 1255 in the British Kingdom.

Richard (Lord) Welert married Eliza Ellen Evens in 1300, place is unknown at this time. She was born in 1279 in the British King …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Richard Wheeler  I於1279年出生在英國時，他的父親Lord Thomas Wielher25歲，母親Terry Lynn Wells25歲。他於1300年與Eliza Ellen Evans在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1365年在他的家鄉享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Richard Wheeler  I于1279年出生在英国时，他的父亲Lord Thomas Wielher25岁，母亲Terry Lynn Wells25岁。他于1300年与Eliza Ellen Evans在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1365年在他的家乡享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wheeler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a wheelwright, a maker of wheels (primarily for carts and other vehicles, but also other kinds of wheels, for use in spinning or other manufacturing processes), from Middle English wheler, whegheler, a derivative of Old English hweogol, hweowol, hwēol ‘wheel’.
History: A founder of Salisbury, NH, in 1634 was John Wheeler.

variants.name: Rower
meanings: English: occupational name from Anglo-Norman French roer ‘wheelwright’ (Old French roier, rouwier, rouer, an agent derivative of roue ‘wheel’). Compare Wheeler .
English: occupational name from Middle English rouer(e) ‘rower, oarsman’. Compare Rother .

variants.name: Wheelwright
meanings: English (Yorkshire): occupational name from Middle English whelewright(e), whelrihte ‘wheelwright, maker of wheels and wheeled vehicles’ (Old English hwēol + wyrhta). See also Wheeler .

History: John Wheelwright (c. 1592–1679), clergyman, came to Boston, MA, from Lincolnshire, England in 1636. He was banished from Massachusettes for his support of his sister-in-law, Anne Hutchinson, in the antinomian controversy; he set up a community at Exeter, NH.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Richard Wheeler  I was born in 1279, in England, his father, Lord Thomas Wielher, was 25 and his mother, Terry Lynn Wells, was 25. He married Eliza Ellen Evans in 1300, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1365, in his hometown, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Richard Wheeler  I est né en 1279, à Angleterre, son père, Lord Thomas Wielher, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Terry Lynn Wells, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Eliza Ellen Evans en 1300, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1365, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Richard Wheeler  I 1279 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lord Thomas Wielher 25 und seine Mutter Terry Lynn Wells 25. Er hat 1300 in England Eliza Ellen Evans geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1365 im Alter von 86 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard Wheeler  I è nato il nel 1279 nel England, il suo padre, Lord Thomas Wielher, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Terry Lynn Wells, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Eliza Ellen Evans nel 1300, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1365, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Richard Wheeler  Iが1279年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Lord Thomas Wielherは25歳，母Terry Lynn Wellsは25歳であった。1300年にイギリスでEliza Ellen Evansと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1365年に故郷で86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Richard Wheeler  I(이)가 1279년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Thomas Wielher(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Terry Lynn Wells(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1300년 영국에서 Eliza Ellen Evans(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1365년 그의 고향에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard Wheeler  I nasceu em 1279, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord Thomas Wielher, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Terry Lynn Wells, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Eliza Ellen Evans em 1300, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1365, em sua cidade natal, com 86 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Richard Wheeler  I родился в 1279 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Lord Thomas Wielher было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Terry Lynn Wells, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Eliza Ellen Evans в 1300 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1365 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 86 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Richard Wheeler  I nació en 1279, en Inglaterra, su papá, Lord Thomas Wielher, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Terry Lynn Wells, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Eliza Ellen Evans en 1300, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1365, en su pueblo, a la edad de 86 años.
